qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload endpoint allows arbitrary file writes into live server directories
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is the insecure default `secureEnabled` configuration in `server/settings.default.js`, which disables authentication globally after installation. This finding instead identifies a route-level flaw: `POST /api/upload` omits `secureFnc` entirely and writes attacker-controlled bytes into live directories, so it is a different vulnerable code path and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause. The advisory describes authentication being disabled by default through the commented-out `secureEnabled` settings path, while the only qualifying finding is a distinct unauthenticated file-write sink in `/api/upload` that lacks its own auth middleware.
evidence: The advisory JSON ties the vulnerability to `server/settings.default.js`, where `secureEnabled` is commented out by default.

In the vulnerable source, `server/api/index.js` initializes auth from `runtime.settings.secureEnabled` and `verifyGroups` returns admin privileges when security is disabled, which is the advisory's global auth-bypass mechanism.
The qualifying finding is centered on `server/api/projects/index.js` `POST /api/upload`, which has no `secureFnc` middleware and directly calls `fs.writeFileSync` on attacker-chosen destinations.
Because `/api/upload` is reachable due to a missing route guard and unsafe write sink rather than the insecure default auth bootstrap, it is an open-world finding rather than the target advisory.
